questions.text: Resources which are present in unlimited quantity in nature and are not likely to be exhausted by human activities are:
questions.explanation: Examples include sunlight and air.

questions.text: Which of the following is an exhaustible natural resource?
questions.explanation: Forests, minerals, coal, petroleum, etc., are limited and can be finished by human use.

questions.text: Fossil fuels are formed from:
questions.explanation: Coal, petroleum and natural gas are formed from the remains of plants and animals buried millions of years ago.

questions.text: The slow process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called:
questions.explanation: Under high pressure and high temperature, dead plants slowly got converted to coal.

questions.text: Which of these is a tough, porous and black substance that is almost a pure form of carbon?
questions.explanation: Coke is used in the manufacture of steel and extraction of many metals.

questions.text: Coal tar is a mixture of about ________ substances.
questions.explanation: Coal tar is a thick black liquid with an unpleasant smell used in various industries.

questions.text: Naphthalene balls, used to repel moths and other insects, are obtained from:
questions.explanation: Coal tar is a source for naphthalene.

questions.text: Which petroleum product is used for surfacing of roads these days in place of coal tar?
questions.explanation: Bitumen is preferred for road surfacing in modern construction.

questions.text: Coal gas was used for street lighting for the first time in ________ in 1810.
questions.explanation: London was the first to use coal gas for street lights.

questions.text: Petroleum was formed from organisms living in the:
questions.explanation: Dead sea organisms settled at the bottom and were covered by layers of sand and clay.

questions.text: The process of separating the various constituents/fractions of petroleum is known as:
questions.explanation: Refining is carried out in a petroleum refinery.

questions.text: Which constituent of petroleum is used as a fuel for heavy motor vehicles and electric generators?
questions.explanation: Diesel is the primary fuel for heavy-duty engines.

questions.text: Which fraction of petroleum is used for making ointments and candles?
questions.explanation: Paraffin wax is a common byproduct used in cosmetics and candles.

questions.text: CNG stands for:
questions.explanation: CNG is used for power generation and as a cleaner fuel for vehicles.

questions.text: Petroleum is also known as 'Black Gold' because of its:
questions.explanation: The many useful substances obtained from petroleum make it very valuable.

questions.text: Natural gas is stored under high pressure as ________.
questions.explanation: CNG is the compressed form of natural gas.

questions.text: Which gas is released when coal is burnt in air?
questions.explanation: Coal contains mainly carbon; burning it produces CO2.

questions.text: PCRA stands for:
questions.explanation: PCRA advises people on how to save petrol/diesel while driving.

questions.text: Where was the world's first oil well drilled?
questions.explanation: The first oil well was drilled in Pennsylvania in 1859.

questions.text: In India, oil is found in:
options: River basins of Godavari and Krishna
questions.explanation: India has several major oil-producing regions.

questions.text: Which of these is not a fossil fuel?
questions.explanation: Wood is a renewable biomass fuel, not a fossil fuel formed over millions of years.

questions.text: LPG stands for:
questions.explanation: LPG is used as fuel for home and industry.

questions.text: Over-exploitation of fossil fuels leads to:
questions.explanation: Burning fossil fuels releases pollutants and CO2, while reserves are limited.

questions.text: Which of the following is used for dry cleaning of clothes?
questions.explanation: Petrol is used as a solvent for dry cleaning.

questions.text: Which gas is used in the manufacture of fertilizers (urea)?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen obtained from natural gas is used in fertilizer production.

questions.text: Fossil fuels cannot be prepared in the laboratory because:
options: Conditions like high pressure/temp cannot be created
The process takes millions of years
questions.explanation: The formation is a geological process requiring specific conditions and vast amounts of time.

questions.text: The main constituent of coal is:
questions.explanation: Coal is primarily made of carbon.

questions.text: Which of these was used as the first fuel for lamps?
questions.explanation: Historically, coal gas was used for lighting before electricity.

questions.text: What is the primary use of Kerosene?
questions.explanation: Kerosene is a versatile fuel used for heating, lighting, and specialized engines.

questions.text: The layer of petroleum and gas is ________ the layer of water in the earth.
questions.explanation: Oil and gas are lighter than water and do not mix with it, so they float above.

questions.text: Which of the following is a byproduct of coal used to make dyes and explosives?
questions.explanation: Coal tar is used as a starting material for synthetic dyes, drugs, and explosives.

questions.text: Coal was formed under which conditions?
questions.explanation: Intense heat and pressure from overlapping soil layers converted dead plants into coal.

questions.text: How long does it take for dead organisms to turn into fossil fuels?
questions.explanation: It is a very slow geological process.

questions.text: Petrochemicals are used in the manufacture of:
questions.explanation: Many synthetic materials are derived from petroleum and natural gas.

questions.text: Which of the following causes the least air pollution?
questions.explanation: CNG is a cleaner fuel compared to liquid or solid fossil fuels.

questions.text: In India, natural gas has been found in:
questions.explanation: India has natural gas reserves across several states and river deltas.

questions.text: To save fuel while driving, PCRA suggests keeping tire pressure ________.
questions.explanation: Correct tire pressure reduces friction and improves fuel efficiency.

questions.text: Fossil fuels are ________ resources.
questions.explanation: Once consumed, they cannot be replenished in a human timescale.

questions.text: Which product is used as a fuel in smelting of iron?
questions.explanation: Coke is used as a reducing agent and fuel in blast furnaces.

questions.text: The dark, oily liquid with an unpleasant odour is:
questions.explanation: This describes crude oil or petroleum as it is extracted.
